paper_title: Adapting CLIP For Phrase Localization Without Further Training

problem_statement.annotation_issues: Annotation process to collect training sets is expensive, labor intensive, and error-prone
problem_statement.pre_trained_biases: Pre-trained components are naturally biased toward few commonly seen object categories, leading to poor performance for uncommon or unseen objects
problem_statement.existing_limitations: ZSGNet remains a supervised approach requiring human annotations on phrase localization

proposed_solution.core_approach: Method that does not rely on any textual grounding, image classification or bounding box annotations
key_components: Leverage CLIP (Contrastive Language-Vision Model) trained on 400M image-text pairs
Extract high-resolution pixel-wise semantic features from images
Compute per-pixel similarity scores with text queries to obtain heatmaps
Localize objects by finding bounding boxes with highest scores
proposed_solution.advantages: Can immediately improve with more advanced CLIP models without extra supervision or training

evaluation.setting: Zero-shot phrase localization following ZSGNet's zero-shot setting
evaluation.results: Outperforms ZSGNet by 5% on three out of four zero-shot splits over Flickr30k and VG
evaluation.comparisons: Achieves comparable performance in long-tailed object categories compared to no-training methods using pre-trained object detectors

contributions: Method for textual grounding entirely from pre-trained language-vision model (CLIP) without bounding box supervision
Methods for extracting high-resolution spatial feature maps from CLIP (for both ViT and ResNet architectures)
Extensive experiments and ablation studies demonstrating effectiveness in zero-shot phrase localization
title: Adapting CLIP For Phrase Localization Without Further Training
